assertions.title: Column Sorting — Ascending/Descending

steps.observed: Table shows highest scores first (0.6560, 0.6156, 0.4694) — correct default order

steps.action: Check for visual sort indicator on initial load
steps.expected: Sort indicator on Overall column showing descending
steps.observed: No visual sort indicator on any column (aria-sort='none' on all). Sort is applied at data level, not via AG Grid sort API.
aria-sort changed to 'ascending'. N/A experiments appear at top of page 1. Note: requires 3 clicks to cycle — none → ascending → descending → none.
aria-sort changed to 'descending'. Highest scores at top (0.6560, 0.6156). Visual sort number indicator shown in header.
aria-sort='ascending' on Experiment, aria-sort='none' on Overall. exp_0000, exp_0001 at top.

steps.action: Sort by Overall descending, scroll to last page
steps.expected: validation_failed and null experiments at bottom
steps.observed: Last page (page 10 of 10) shows exp_lme_0071 (N/A, validation_failed). Page 9 bottom shows exp_lme_0063, exp_lme_0066, exp_lme_0067, exp_lme_0070 (all N/A, validation_failed). Correctly sorted to bottom.

evidence.network: Sort is client-side (AG Grid), no network requests

assertions.issues: 1) No visual sort indicator on initial load (aria-sort='none' on all columns). The default sort is pre-applied at the data level, not via AG Grid's sort API, so no sort arrow appears until user explicitly clicks a header. 2) Sort cycling is: none → ascending → descending → none (3-state cycle instead of the typical 2-state asc/desc toggle). First click goes from data-order to ascending (not descending), which is counterintuitive given the data appears pre-sorted descending.

steps.expected: validation_failed (N/A) and null experiments sort to bottom
steps.observed: On descending sort, N/A experiments consistently appear at bottom. Page 9 bottom: exp_lme_0063 (validation_failed), exp_lme_0066 (validation_failed), exp_lme_0067 (validation_failed), exp_lme_0070 (validation_failed). Page 10 (last): exp_lme_0071 (validation_failed).

steps.expected: N/A experiments at top without crash
steps.observed: N/A experiments appear at top when sorted ascending. No JavaScript errors.

steps.action: Check for JS errors during sort operations
steps.observed: No console errors before or after sorting.
145ms (cold load via performance.timing), 50ms (cached). Well under 3s threshold.
~248ms (measured via MutationObserver for grid content change). Well under 2s.
~350ms (estimated: ~500ms total click+wait+eval minus ~150ms eval overhead). Under 1s.
~350ms (estimated same as sort). Under 1s.
~628ms (1128ms total minus 500ms wait). Under 2s.
Table uses pagination (50 rows per page, 10 pages total). Page change takes ~200ms. Smooth interaction.
Pagination buttons work correctly. Page size selector available (25, 50, 100, 200). No jank observed.

evidence.network: All operations are client-side after initial data load. Dash callbacks respond in 4-6ms.
evidence.performanceNotes: Measurements taken on localhost (no network latency). Performance timing methodology: rAF + eval for sort/filter; MutationObserver for era switch; performance.timing for page load. agent-browser command overhead ~160ms per eval, ~324ms per click+eval pair.

assertions.title: URL State Management

steps.action: Set era to LongMemEval via dropdown
steps.observed: URL stays at http://localhost:8050/ with no query parameters. Era filter applies correctly (93 experiments shown) but URL does not reflect the change.

steps.action: Click experiment row to open detail view
steps.observed: URL stays at http://localhost:8050/. Detail view opens correctly but URL does not update. Inconsistent behavior: sometimes URL briefly shows ?experiment=exp_lme_0077 but is immediately stripped.

steps.expected: View restored with LongMemEval era and exp_lme_0008 detail view
steps.observed: PARTIAL SUCCESS: The era filter shows LongMemEval and detail view shows exp_lme_0008 correctly. However, the URL is immediately stripped to http://localhost:8050/ (query params consumed but not preserved).

steps.action: Refresh page after setting era and opening detail view
steps.observed: FAIL: After reload, URL is bare http://localhost:8050/. Detail view state is preserved via dcc.Store (client-side storage), but only if the browser doesn't clear session storage. URL-based restoration fails because the URL never contains the state.

steps.action: Test browser back button from detail view
steps.observed: FAIL: Browser back navigates to previous agent-browser-opened URL, not to the leaderboard. Since the URL never changes when opening/closing detail views, browser history has no entries for view state changes.
FAIL: Same issue — no URL-based history entries for view changes, so forward doesn't restore detail view.

evidence.network: URL state management code exists in app.py (update_url_state, restore_from_url callbacks) but the dcc.Location component with refresh=False appears to strip query params immediately after they are set.

assertions.issues:
The URL state management is fundamentally broken. Three specific failures:
1) Era filter changes do NOT update the URL — URL stays at http://localhost:8050/ regardless of era selection.
2) Experiment detail view opening does NOT consistently update the URL — sometimes ?experiment=<id> appears briefly but is immediately stripped.
3) Browser back/forward do NOT work for view state changes because the URL never changes.
The restore_from_url callback DOES work (navigating directly to ?era=LongMemEval&experiment=exp_lme_0008 correctly restores the view), but since the update_url_state callback fails to maintain URL params, bookmarkability and back/forward navigation are non-functional.
Root cause: dcc.Location(refresh=False) may be stripping query params, or the update_url_state callback's output to Output('url', 'search') is not being applied by Dash.

frictions.description: Row clicks to open detail view are unreliable — sometimes the click doesn't register with the AG Grid cellClicked event. Need a 1+ second wait after page load before clicks work, and even then behavior is inconsistent across attempts. The enableCellTextSelection: True AG Grid option may be interfering with click events.
frictions.resolution: Worked around by adding 1s wait after page load before attempting row clicks. The detail view eventually opens after 2-3 click attempts.

frictions.description: AG Grid pagination buttons cannot be clicked via agent-browser refs (click @e9 for Next Page doesn't work). Must use JavaScript (document.querySelectorAll('.ag-paging-button').find(b => b.getAttribute('aria-label') === 'Next Page').click()) to trigger pagination.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to click pagination buttons directly via DOM API.

frictions.description: Sort indicator has a 3-state cycle (none → ascending → descending → none) instead of the typical 2-state (ascending ↔ descending). The initial 'none' state means no sort arrow is shown even though data is pre-sorted by overall_score descending.
frictions.resolution: Noted as a finding. First click on Overall goes to ascending (N/A values first), second click goes to descending (highest values first), third click returns to 'none' (data-order).

frictions.description: Performance timing via agent-browser is imprecise due to tool overhead (~160ms per eval, ~324ms per click+eval pair). Used MutationObserver and rAF for more accurate measurements.
frictions.resolution: Used multiple timing approaches: performance.timing for page load, MutationObserver for era switch, rAF for sort/filter timing, wall-clock subtraction for detail view.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-TABLE-006), 1 partially passed (VAL-TABLE-005), 1 passed (VAL-TABLE-018), 1 failed (VAL-TABLE-021). VAL-TABLE-005 partial because default sort has no visual indicator and sort cycling is 3-state instead of 2-state. VAL-TABLE-021 failed because URL state management is broken — era filter and experiment selection do not update the browser URL, making bookmarkability and browser back/forward non-functional.
